questions.question: How does the WHO ICD-11 classify burnout?

options.text: As a medical diagnosis under mental health disorders
As an occupational phenomenon under 'Factors influencing health status' — not a medical diagnosis
As a type of major depressive disorder

questions.explanation: The WHO classifies burnout as an occupational phenomenon under 'Factors influencing health status or contact with health services' (QD85) — NOT as a medical diagnosis. This means burnout describes a work-generated syndrome requiring primarily organizational responses (change the work conditions), not just individual treatment.

questions.question: What are the three dimensions of burnout according to the WHO definition?

options.text: Emotional exhaustion, cynicism/depersonalization, and reduced professional efficacy
Workload, time pressure, and role conflict
Physical illness, mental illness, and social withdrawal

questions.explanation: Burnout has three specific dimensions: (1) emotional exhaustion (energy depletion), (2) cynicism/mental distance/depersonalization (psychological withdrawal from work), and (3) reduced professional efficacy (self-doubt about competence). Exhaustion is the most empirically robust core dimension.

questions.question: What is the key difference between burnout and major depressive disorder?

options.text: Burnout is more severe than depression
Burnout is work-context-specific; depression is pervasive across all life domains
Burnout affects only physical health; depression affects only mental health
There is no difference — they are the same condition

questions.explanation: Burnout is work-context-specific — symptoms occur primarily in relation to your job. Depression is pervasive across all life domains — you lose interest and pleasure in most activities, including things you used to love outside of work. The two can co-occur, and burnout is a risk factor for developing depression.

questions.question: When should you seek professional evaluation for burnout?

options.text: Only if you have a diagnosed mental health condition
Never — burnout is something you should manage on your own
If you experience exhaustion not relieved by rest, functional impairment, symptoms lasting 4-6+ weeks, pervasive low mood, or thoughts of self-harm
Only if your supervisor recommends it

questions.explanation: Severe burnout warrants professional evaluation. Seek help if you experience: exhaustion not relieved by rest, functional impairment (difficulty completing work or daily tasks), symptoms lasting 4-6+ weeks without improvement, pervasive low mood or loss of pleasure (possible depression), or any thoughts of self-harm or suicide. Burnout's exhaustion dimension is independently associated with suicidal ideation.

title: What Burnout Is (and Isn't) — WHO Classification - Quiz
